title: Contributing to coursepackR
text: Thank interest improving coursepackR! document explains report issues, suggest changes, contribute code.

title: Contributing to coursepackR
text: something isn’t working, please open issue GitHub. helpful bug report includes: R command ran exact error message Output mccourse_check() (can see environment) R version (R.version.string) operating system Steps reproduce problem

title: Contributing to coursepackR
text: Feature requests welcome! Open GitHub issue describing: ’d like package useful students instructors ideas might work

title: Contributing to coursepackR
text: Fork repository GitHub. Clone fork locally: Install development dependencies: Load package interactive development:
code: git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/coursepackR.git cd coursepackR install.packages(c("devtools", "roxygen2", "testthat", "pkgdown")) devtools::load_all()

title: Contributing to coursepackR
text: Documentation: Use roxygen2 exported functions. Include @param, @return, @examples, @export tags. User-facing messages: Use cli package (cli::cli_alert_success(), cli::cli_alert_danger(), etc.) — message(), cat(), print(). Errors warnings: Use rlang::abort() rlang::warn() instead stop() warning(). Tests: Use testthat (edition 3). Put tests tests/testthat/test-*.R. File paths: Always use file.path() — never paste paths / \\.

title: Contributing to coursepackR
text: Create branch changes: Make edits R/, man/, vignettes/, tests/. Rebuild documentation changed roxygen2 comments: Run tests: Run R CMD check: Aim 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes. Commit push: Open pull request GitHub main branch. Describe changed .
code: git checkout -b fix/describe-your-change devtools::document() devtools::test() devtools::check() git add -A git commit -m "Brief description of change" git push origin fix/describe-your-change

title: Contributing to coursepackR
text: devtools::check() passes errors warnings devtools::test() passes tests New exported functions roxygen2 documentation New features least one test Vignettes build without errors

title: Contributing to coursepackR
text: Open issue contact maintainer aleith@siue.edu.

title: Getting Started
text: week follows pattern:

title: Getting Started
text: returns sorted vector week numbers (1 17).

title: Getting Started
text: copies week’s .qmd assignment template current working directory folder named week_01/. folder contains single Quarto document week’s assignment. folder already exists, function stops message prevent overwriting work. Delete rename old folder want fresh copy.

title: Getting Started
text: opens week_01/ folder system file browser (Finder Mac, Explorer Windows). , double-click .qmd file open RStudio.

title: Getting Started
text: Open .qmd file RStudio. template includes: YAML header name, date, week’s title Reading prompts tied textbook chapter Code chunks R exercises Reflection questions Fill responses, run code chunks, render document ’re done (click Render button press Ctrl+Shift+K).

title: Getting Started
text: downloads reflective journal template. file automatically named current date (e.g., journal_2026-02-19.qmd) entry gets file. journal template offers three thinking paths — pick one fits experience week: Connector — relate already know? Troubleshooter — problems solve? Critic — match experience?

title: Getting Started
text: package bundles unified_music dataset — 1,792 songs Billboard chart data, Spotify audio features, Genius metadata.

title: Getting Started
text: dataset lazy-loaded, meaning ’s available soon call data() without reading disk time.

title: Getting Started
code: # Dimensions dim(unified_music) #> [1] 1792   36  # Column names names(unified_music)  # First few rows head(unified_music)  # Summary of a numeric variable summary(unified_music$danceability)

title: Getting Started
text: complete variable reference types, ranges, descriptions, see vignette("unified-music-codebook") Data Dictionary appendix textbook.

title: Getting Started
text: reflective journal weekly habit. call download_journal() creates new file: Journal entries accumulate working directory. end semester, ’ll 15–17 entries documenting growth researcher.
code: # Download this week's journal download_journal("mc451")  # The file is named with today's date # e.g., journal_2026-02-19.qmd

title: Getting Started
text: reach end semester, scaffold final project: creates folder called mc451_final_project/ : Open .Rproj file RStudio start working clean project environment. Quarto document includes structured outline paper (introduction, methods, results, discussion).

title: Getting Started
text: chapters bundled package markdown files:
code: # List all chapters list.files(system.file("textbook", package = "coursepackR"))  # Path to a specific chapter ch1 <- system.file("textbook", "chapter01.md", package = "coursepackR")  # Read in RStudio file.edit(ch1)

title: Getting Started
text: downloading materials, set working directory course folder. RStudio: Session > Set Working Directory > Choose Directory. R: download_week() download_journal() files go folder.

title: Getting Started
text: Create RStudio Project course (File > New Project > Existing Directory). keeps working directory consistent across sessions makes easy find files.

title: Getting Started
text: visit function reference pkgdown site.

title: Installation & Setup
text: installing coursepackR, need: R (>= 4.1) — Download https://cran.r-project.org/ RStudio Desktop (recommended) — Download https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/ need step--step installation instructions R RStudio, see R & RStudio Installation Guide course website. Check R version: version 4.1, download latest R proceeding.

title: Installation & Setup
text: coursepackR hosted GitHub. Install remotes package: downloads installs package along dependencies (cli, rlang, utils). install takes 1–2 minutes connections.
code: # Install remotes if you don't have it install.packages("remotes")  # Install coursepackR remotes::install_github("SIM-Lab-SIUE/coursepackR")

title: Installation & Setup
text: “package called ‘remotes’” — Run install.packages("remotes") first. “open URL” timeout errors — Check internet connection. ’re campus corporate network, firewall may blocking GitHub. Try personal network ask allow github.com api.github.com. “package ‘coursepackR’ available version R” — need R >= 4.1. Update R try .

title: Installation & Setup
text: installing, run one-time setup function: function walks several steps: Quarto CLI — Checks whether Quarto installed. , opens download page (https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/) pauses can install . Quarto required rendering assignment templates HTML PDF. TinyTeX — Installs TinyTeX, lightweight LaTeX distribution needed PDF output. longest step (5–10 minutes first run). already LaTeX distribution, step skipped. Core R packages — Installs packages used course assignments, including tidyverse, knitr, rmarkdown, others. Packages already skipped. step prints status messages can see ’s happening. step fails, function reports error continues remaining steps.

title: Installation & Setup
text: Restart RStudio running mccourse_setup(). ensures Quarto new packages properly detected.

title: Installation & Setup
text: Run diagnostic check confirm everything working: returns named list status component: passing check shows green checkmark. failing check shows red X suggested fix.

title: Installation & Setup
text: R version — Download latest R https://cran.r-project.org/ reinstall coursepackR. RStudio — Download https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/. recommended package works without . Quarto — Download https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/. installing, restart RStudio re-check. Git — Download https://git-scm.com/. installing, restart RStudio. See GitHub Setup Guide configuration. Templates — always pass. fails, reinstall package remotes::install_github("SIM-Lab-SIUE/coursepackR").

title: Installation & Setup
text: Check updates time: compares installed version latest release GitHub. update available, ask whether want install . can also force reinstall:

text: remove coursepackR: remove TinyTeX packages installed mccourse_setup(). remove TinyTeX separately:

title: The Unified Music Dataset
text: dataset contains 1,792 songs 36 variables drawn three public sources: Billboard Hot 100 — Weekly chart rankings, weeks chart, chart entry/exit dates Spotify Web API — Audio features (danceability, energy, valence, etc.), track metadata, playlist classifications Genius — Page view counts, featured artists, lyrics text

title: The Unified Music Dataset
text: dataset lazy-loaded compressed xz, ’s available immediately data() minimal memory overhead.

title: The Unified Music Dataset
text: Note: Lower values Max_Rank indicate better performance (1 top position).

title: The Unified Music Dataset
text: examples show common operations ’ll perform course.

title: The Unified Music Dataset
code: # Correlation between danceability and energy cor(unified_music$danceability, unified_music$energy, use = "complete.obs")  # Scatter plot plot(unified_music$danceability, unified_music$energy,      xlab = "Danceability", ylab = "Energy",      main = "Danceability vs. Energy",      pch = 20, col = rgb(0, 0, 0, 0.2))

title: The Unified Music Dataset
code: # Average max rank by genre (lower = better) aggregate(Max_Rank ~ playlist_genre, data = unified_music, FUN = mean)

title: The Unified Music Dataset
code: # Convert milliseconds to minutes unified_music$duration_min <- unified_music$duration_ms / 60000 summary(unified_music$duration_min)

title: The Unified Music Dataset
text: songs appear multiple Spotify playlists may different playlist_genre playlist_subgenre values. lyrics may NA songs Genius lyrics available. views reflects page views time data collection may reflect current counts. track_popularity point--time Spotify metric changes time. Billboard chart dates use character format (e.g., “2020-03-14”) can converted .Date().

title: The Unified Music Dataset
text: Data Dictionary appendix textbook Chapter 9 (Vibes Variables) — operationalizing music variables Chapter 12 (Wrangling Chaos) — data cleaning preparation Chapter 13 (Seeing Patterns) — exploratory data analysis ?unified_music — roxygen2 help page full variable list

what: coursepackR - Course Tools for From Vibes to Variables
title: coursepackR - Course Tools for From Vibes to Variables
text: Student-facing R package MC-451 Research Methods SIUE, built around open textbook Vibes Variables: Field Guide Open Media Science.

title: coursepackR - Course Tools for From Vibes to Variables
text: Requires R >= 4.1. RStudio Desktop recommended required.
code: # Install remotes if you don't have it install.packages("remotes")  # Install coursepackR from GitHub remotes::install_github("SIM-Lab-SIUE/coursepackR")

title: coursepackR - Course Tools for From Vibes to Variables
code: library(coursepackR)  # 1. First-time setup (installs Quarto, TinyTeX, core packages) mccourse_setup()  # 2. Verify your environment mccourse_check()  # 3. Download this week's materials download_week("mc451", 1)  # 4. Open the folder in your file browser open_week(1)  # 5. Access the music dataset data(unified_music) head(unified_music)

title: coursepackR - Course Tools for From Vibes to Variables
text: unified_music dataset contains 1,792 songs 36 variables three sources: Billboard — chart rank, weeks board, chart entry/exit dates Spotify — audio features (danceability, energy, valence, tempo, loudness, etc.) Genius — page views, lyrics, featured artists See Unified Music Dataset vignette full variable reference example analyses.

title: coursepackR - Course Tools for From Vibes to Variables
text: 17 chapters Vibes Variables bundled offline reading: Read full book online https://sim-lab-siue.github.io/vibes--variables/.

title: coursepackR - Course Tools for From Vibes to Variables
text: 17 weeks .qmd assignment templates MC-451, aligned textbook chapters:

title: coursepackR - Course Tools for From Vibes to Variables
text: week semester: Download week’s template: Open folder: Work .qmd file RStudio — read chapter, answer prompts, run code Render HTML PDF finished (click Render RStudio quarto render) Reflect journal:

title: coursepackR - Course Tools for From Vibes to Variables
text: “Error loadNamespace: package called ‘remotes’” Run install.packages("remotes") first, retry install. “package ‘coursepackR’ available version R” need R >= 4.1. Check version R.version.string. Download latest R https://cran.r-project.org/. mccourse_setup() says Quarto found Download Quarto CLI https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/. installing, restart RStudio. mccourse_check() shows failing check Run mccourse_setup() install missing components, re-check. download_week() says folder already exists function won’t overwrite existing files. Delete rename old folder want fresh copy.

title: coursepackR - Course Tools for From Vibes to Variables
code: coursepackR/ ├── R/                    # Package functions ├── man/                  # Auto-generated help pages ├── data/                 # unified_music.rda (lazy-loaded) ├── data-raw/             # Source CSV and preparation script ├── inst/ │   ├── textbook/         # 17 markdown chapters (offline access) │   └── courses/mc451/ │       ├── weeks/        # Weekly .qmd templates (week_01–week_17) │       └── journal/      # Journal template ├── vignettes/            # Long-form guides ├── tests/testthat/       # Unit tests └── .github/workflows/    # CI/CD (R CMD check, pkgdown)

title: coursepackR - Course Tools for From Vibes to Variables
text: MIT

title: Download Journal Entry Template — download_journal
text: Creates week-specific copy journal template. call produces uniquely named file (e.g., Week_03_Journal.qmd) previous entries never overwritten.

title: Download Journal Entry Template — download_journal
text: week Integer week number (1–17). course Character course identifier (default: "mc451"). dest Character destination directory (default: current directory). journal/ subdirectory created automatically.

title: Download Journal Entry Template — download_journal
text: Invisibly, path created file.

title: Download Weekly Course Materials — download_week
text: Copies week's template files installed package local directory. Creates week_XX subdirectory destination.

title: Download Weekly Course Materials — download_week
text: course Character course identifier (e.g., "mc451"). one course installed, can integer week number convenience. week Integer character week number. Accepts 1, "01", "week_01" formats. dest Character destination directory (default: current directory).

title: Download Weekly Course Materials — download_week
text: Invisibly, list components dest, course, week, n_copied.

title: Create Final Project Folder — final_project
text: Scaffolds final project directory Quarto document, bibliography, data folder, RStudio project file.

title: Create Final Project Folder — final_project
text: course Character course identifier (e.g., "mc451", "mc501"). dest Character parent directory (default: current directory).

title: Create Final Project Folder — final_project
text: Invisibly, path created project folder.

title: List Available Courses — list_courses
text: Returns character vector course identifiers installed weekly template materials.

title: List Available Courses — list_courses
text: Character vector course names (e.g., "mc451"), empty vector courses installed.

title: List Available Weeks — list_weeks
text: Returns week identifiers available given course, across courses course specified.

title: List Available Weeks — list_weeks
text: course Character course identifier (e.g., "mc451"). NULL, returns union weeks across installed courses.

title: List Available Weeks — list_weeks
text: Character vector week identifiers (e.g., "week_01", "week_02"), sorted numerically.

title: Check Course Environment — mccourse_check
text: Runs series diagnostic checks verify student's environment properly configured. Checks R version, RStudio, Quarto CLI, Git, installed course templates.

title: Check Course Environment — mccourse_check
text: course Character course identifier check templates (default: "mc451"). min_r Character minimum R version required (default: "4.1.0").

title: Check Course Environment — mccourse_check
text: named list logical results check: r, rstudio, quarto, git, templates.

title: One-Time Environment Setup — mccourse_setup
text: Installs Quarto CLI support, TinyTeX PDF rendering, core R packages needed course. Safe run multiple times; installs missing components.

title: One-Time Environment Setup — mccourse_setup
text: Invisibly, TRUE.

title: Check for Package Updates — mccourse_update
text: Checks whether newer version coursepackR available GitHub optionally installs .

title: Check for Package Updates — mccourse_update
code: mccourse_update(   update = c("ask", "always", "never"),   repo = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Check for Package Updates — mccourse_update
text: update Character: "ask" (default) prompts interactively, "always" updates without asking, "never" checks. repo Character GitHub repository "owner/repo" format. Defaults getOption("coursepackR.repo", "SIM-Lab-SIUE/coursepackR"). quiet Logical; TRUE, suppress informational messages.

title: Check for Package Updates — mccourse_update
text: Invisibly, TRUE update performed, FALSE otherwise.

what: Open a Downloaded Week Folder — open_week
title: Open a Downloaded Week Folder — open_week
text: Opens specified week's folder system file browser (Finder, File Explorer, etc.).

title: Open a Downloaded Week Folder — open_week
Open a Downloaded Week Folder — open_week
text: week Integer character week number. dest Character parent directory week folders downloaded (default: current directory).

title: Open a Downloaded Week Folder — open_week
text: Invisibly, path opened.

title: Open a Downloaded Week Folder — open_week
Unified Music Dataset — unified_music
text: curated dataset combining Billboard chart performance, Spotify audio features, Genius metadata 1,792 songs. Used throughout "Vibes Variables" teaching data analysis research methods.

title: Unified Music Dataset — unified_music
text: data frame 1,792 rows 36 variables: Song Song title Artist Artist name Average_Rank Average Billboard chart position Max_Rank Highest Billboard chart position achieved (1 = top) First_Week_on_Board Date first chart appearance First_Rank_on_Board Chart position first appearance Last_Week_on_Board Date last chart appearance Last_Rank_on_Board Chart position last appearance Weeks_on_Board Number weeks Billboard chart track_id Spotify track identifier track_popularity Spotify popularity score (0-100) track_album_id Spotify album identifier track_album_name Album title track_album_release_date Album release date playlist_name Source Spotify playlist playlist_id Spotify playlist identifier playlist_genre Genre category playlist_subgenre Subgenre category danceability Spotify danceability score (0-1) energy Spotify energy score (0-1) key Musical key (0-11, 0 = C) loudness Overall loudness decibels mode Modality (0 = minor, 1 = major) speechiness Spotify speechiness score (0-1) acousticness Spotify acousticness score (0-1) instrumentalness Spotify instrumentalness score (0-1) liveness Spotify liveness score (0-1) valence Spotify positivity/happiness score (0-1) tempo Tempo beats per minute duration_ms Track duration milliseconds tag Content tag label year Release year views Genius page view count features Featured artists lyrics Song lyrics text id Genius song identifier

title: Unified Music Dataset — unified_music
text: Compiled Billboard, Spotify Web API, Genius API data. See textbook full methodology.
